body.title: [sprint] Prospective pre-registration on live sprints — the durable ground-truth spine
body.description:
**Problem:** Retrospective replay grounds the past; the thesis is about the future. The durable steady-state ground-truth spine is pre-registration on live tickets — but only if the GRADER is uncoupled too, else it silently falls back to proof-on-close and re-imports the coupling the whole program removes (advisor 2026-07-05).

**Proposed shape:** Before each live sprint runs, the compiled path writes its prediction — warm/cold, which files, what plan — to an append-only pre-registration file, timestamped, BEFORE the ticket is worked. After the ticket ships, grade the prediction using the SAME verdict_strength machinery from T-corpus-verdict-attachment (uncoupled grader), NOT proof-on-close. Each future ticket becomes a fresh, unspent, un-tunable held-out test at zero marginal cost.

**Affected files:** NEW unseen_university/devices/inference/prereg.py (append-only prediction writer + grader that calls corpus_verdict); a hook point in the sprint path (sprint-ticket) that fires the pre-write before work begins — fail-soft, must never break a sprint. The sprint-path hook is the load-bearing touch — name its docstring.

**Design rules:** flat-file append-only; grader REUSES corpus_verdict (no duplicate verdict logic); fail-soft hook; docs-live-in-code on the sprint hook.

**Scope boundary:** pre-write + reality-graded scoring of predictions only. Does NOT change how sprints select/route work; does NOT block a sprint on prediction outcome.

**Test plan:** red→green. Simulate a sprint: assert the prediction is written to the append-only file BEFORE the work-simulating step. KEY proof: mock proof-on-close to return a bogus PASS and assert the grade IGNORES it and uses the reality verdict from verdict_strength — RED on a naive grader that trusts proof-on-close, GREEN on the uncoupled grader.

**Logging:** the fail-soft sprint hook must log.warning('prereg: pre-write failed (non-fatal): %s', exc) on any exception so a swallowed pre-write is visible. **Observable via:** log.info('prereg: prediction written ticket=%s warm=%s files=%d') before work begins; grep the prereg file to confirm one record per live sprint. **Rollback:** remove the single hook call in the sprint path — prereg is additive and fail-soft, so removal restores prior sprint behavior exactly.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

**Completion criteria:** prereg.py writes one prediction record (warm/cold, files, plan) to the append-only file BEFORE work begins on a sprint, and grades it via corpus_verdict.verdict_strength; a bogus proof-on-close PASS is ignored by the grader in a red->green test; the sprint hook is fail-soft.
body.intention: I intend that every future live ticket becomes a fresh held-out test the system cannot tune, graded by an uncoupled verdict rather than by proof-on-close.
